Good, the game reset. Now I see the full path again with magenta at the purple square position. Let me check the exact positions and then try a different approach.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['M', 'W', 'G'] and n['pixels'] < 20:
        b = n['boundary']
        r_min, r_max = min(r for r,c in b), max(r for r,c in b)
        c_min, c_max = min(c for r,c in b), max(c for r,c in b)
        print(f"color={n['color']} px={n['pixels']} center=({(r_min+r_max)//2},{(c_min+c_max)//2})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now after reset:
- White (W, 12px) at (21, 39) - the ring center is filled white
- Magenta (M, 1px) at (40, 33) - on the path
- Dark gray (G, 12px) at (59, 27) - at the end of path
So the white is at the START of the path (ring center) and dark gray is at the END of the path. The magenta is on the path between them.
When I advance the magenta, it moves toward the dark gray. The white follows behind.
Maybe the goal is to have the magenta reach the dark gray square. But the path ends at the purple square, not at the dark gray square.
Wait, let me think about this differently. The path goes: ring (white) → purple square → dark gray square. When magenta advances, it moves along this path. The white follows. When magenta reaches the purple square, the path disappears.
Maybe I need to swap white and dark gray BEFORE advancing, so the dark gray is at the ring position and white is at the end. Then when magenta advances, it would end up at... hmm, that doesn't make sense either.
Let me try a different approach: swap white and dark gray first, then advance magenta all the way.
